problem_summary: 2022 MCM Problem A (continuous): model a cyclist's power output over time and derive an optimal pacing/power-distribution strategy that minimizes total time on individual time-trial courses, then extend to a six-rider team time trial (TTT). The paper builds a physiology-based power-duration model, a force/energy dynamics model over a discretized course (1164 sections), and optimizes the per-section power profile with simulated annealing; it then does sensitivity analysis on wind and on power-execution deviations, extends to TTT with drafting, and writes a 2-page Directeur Sportif guidance.
models_used: Model I - Critical Power / Anaerobic Work Capacity (AWC) physiology model: dw/dt = Pc - P, with hyperbolic power-duration curve P = (alpha*AWC + alpha*Pc*T + Pc)/(1 + alpha*T); fitted to Pinot & Grappe (2011) data giving alpha=0.0399/s, Pc=354.5W, AWC=24010J

Power-limit submodel: P_lim = alpha*w + Pc making instantaneous max power a finite function of remaining anaerobic energy; over-limit work W_ex attenuates alpha via alpha(W_ex)=alpha(0)-1e-5*alpha*W_ex (fatigue/over-exertion penalty)

Model II - Newtonian force/energy dynamics per course section: power balance P_eff - aero drag - rolling friction - dEp/dt - dEk/dt = 0, reduced to ODE dv/ds*v = a/v + b*v^2 + c, integrated numerically to get section velocity and time

Aerodynamic drag with wind as a vector model: F = -0.5*rho*|v_r|^2*C_D*A*(v_r/|v_r|), projected onto direction of motion using azimuth angles phi (wind) and gamma (motion) in an E-N coordinate frame so tail/head/cross winds are signed

Cornering / no-skid constraint model: circular-motion centripetal force f_n = m*v^2/R with helix-equivalent radius R = r*sqrt(1+tan^2(theta)), combined-friction limit sqrt(f_n^2+f_tau^2) <= mu_s*m*g yielding a per-section safe-speed cap v_safe

Simulated Annealing optimization: decision variables = per-section power P_i (1164 vars), objective min total time sum t_i subject to energy (0<=w<=AWC), velocity (v<=v_safe), and total-energy constraints; Metropolis acceptance exp(-(f1-f0)/T), geometric cooling K_{j+1}=a*K_j

TTT extension - drafting/permutation model: per-section drag coefficient selected from rider permutations (6!=720, reduced to 4!=24 after dropping two windbreakers) via binary selection vectors; SA jointly optimizes drop-point i_d, power profile P_i, and formation A_i with objective on the 4th finisher's time

Image-based course digitization: Python pixel extraction of topographic maps to recover per-section slope/elevation; self-designed course generated via cubic spline interpolation of normally distributed elevation points
key_techniques: Two-tier modeling that cleanly separates physiology (what power a rider CAN produce) from mechanics (what that power DOES on the road), then couples them through the SA optimizer

Literature-grounded parameter calibration: alpha, Pc, AWC, lambda=0.977, mu=0.0025, S_eff (0.304 standing / 0.245 seated) all sourced from named cited papers (Pinot & Grappe, Martin 2006) rather than guessed
Reformulating the time-integral as an ODE in distance s (dv/ds*v form) so per-section velocity and section time can be solved numerically section-by-section with v_i as boundary condition

Constrained SA with a feasibility filter inside the perturbation loop - disturbing 100 sections per step and rejecting any disturbance that violates energy/velocity constraints, keeping the search inside the feasible region

Encoding discrete combinatorial choices (formation permutation, windbreaker drop section) as additional SA decision variables alongside the continuous power vector - a hybrid discrete-continuous optimization
Coarse-to-fine search for the drop point i_d: evaluate 10 discrete candidates, narrow to the sub-peak interval, then add i_d to the SA decision set - taming a large mixed search space

Inverse sensitivity use: rather than only reporting sensitivity, they invert it - from a tolerated 0.3% performance loss they back-solve allowable split-time windows for key sections (e.g. 11-13km: 337-401s), turning analysis into actionable race targets

Defining course-geometry-dependent sensitivity: showing circular/loop courses (course1) are nearly wind-insensitive while north-south winding courses (course2) are strongly wind-sensitive - a genuinely insightful, non-obvious result
why_it_won: "From a judge's view this paper wins because it converts a vague 'find the best strategy' prompt into a fully closed-loop, physically faithful pipeline: a calibrated physiology model feeds a Newtonian dynamics model feeds a constrained global optimizer, and every block is justified, parameterized from cited literature, and validated on real courses (Tokyo Olympic, UCI Worlds) plus a stress-test self-designed course. The 29%/9%/18% improvement-vs-constant-power baseline gives a concrete, believable quantitative payoff. The sensitivity analysis is unusually mature - it not only perturbs wind and execution noise but ties results to course geometry and then inverts the analysis into split-time targets. The extracted strategies (push uphill, recover downhill, alternate on flats, final sprint) are exactly what real cycling coaches do, signaling the model recovers ground truth. The TTT extension genuinely adds new physics (drafting) and a new decision variable (drop point) rather than rehashing Model II. Finally the writing is disciplined: clear notation table, justified assumptions, and a persuasive, jargon-light 2-page Directeur Sportif letter that even addresses real-world execution (power sensors, headset coaching)."
section_highlights: "Summary front-loads the modeling arc and names the workhorse method (SA) in the keywords. A flowchart (Figure 1) maps the whole solution before details begin. Seven explicitly justified assumptions and a full notation table establish rigor early. The modeling section builds derivations step-by-step from dw/dt to the closed-form power-duration curve, then to the dv/ds ODE, with each equation numbered and motivated. Results are anchored to a constant-power baseline so improvements are quantified (29%/9%/18%). Sensitivity analysis pairs per-section sensitivity plots against the elevation profile to visually prove climbs/turns are the critical sections. The Strengths/Weaknesses list is honest (admits SA non-global, per-course tuning, data dependence). The closing Directeur Sportif letter translates math into plain coaching language and pre-empts the practical objection that riders cannot feel their wattage by proposing onboard sensors and real-time headset feedback."
pitfalls_or_limitations: SA gives no global-optimality guarantee and requires re-tuning parameters (initial/termination temperature, cooling rate) per course - the authors admit this; reproducibility and stability across runs are unaddressed (they only smooth the noisy power output for plotting)

Heavy dependence on quoted literature parameters with no independent validation; Assumption 7 ('the data is accurate') is essentially an escape hatch and the model is self-described as 'highly sensitive to the accuracy of quoted data'

Several physical simplifications are strong: wind assumed parallel to motion (Assumption 3), constant recovery rate, all team members identical (Assumption 4), instantaneous formation changes, constant safe inter-rider distance - real TTT dynamics (acceleration during pulls, variable draft fraction) are not modeled

Document has noticeable polish gaps: multiple typos (Assuptions, dierctions), a duplicated/garbled constant lambda=97.7% / 097.7%, and the over-limit attenuation coefficient (1e-5) and the W_ex mechanism are asserted with no calibration source

Validation is qualitative/illustrative: only one synthetic baseline (constant Pc power) is compared; no comparison to actual recorded race times, no error bars, no cross-validation of the physiology fit
The drafting coefficients c_jk for each of the 720/24 permutations are referenced but never given numerically, so the TTT result is not fully reproducible from the paper
The safe-speed equation (21) appears dimensionally/algebraically garbled (a 4th-root expression mixing force and radius terms), suggesting transcription errors that a judge could catch
Mermaid SA flowchart has an inconsistent edge (the 'no' branch routing to End vs Output), a minor logic-presentation slip
reusable_patterns: Physiology-to-mechanics-to-optimizer pipeline: separate 'capacity' model from 'effect' model and bridge them with a constrained optimizer - reusable for any sports/effort-allocation or resource-pacing problem

Discretize a continuous domain (course) into N sections, solve a local ODE per section with the previous section's state as boundary condition, and sum local times/costs to get a global objective - a general spatial-marching numerical pattern

Constrained simulated annealing with an in-loop feasibility filter (reject infeasible perturbations) - a clean template for optimization under hard physical constraints when the feasible set is awkward

Hybrid discrete+continuous SA: fold combinatorial choices (orderings, switch-points) into the decision vector, and use coarse-to-fine bracketing for an expensive discrete variable before refining - reusable for scheduling/formation problems
Baseline-relative reporting: always quantify improvement against a naive constant/greedy baseline (here constant-Pc power) to make the model's value concrete and judge-legible
Inverse sensitivity: convert a tolerated performance-loss budget into allowable operating windows (split-time ranges) - turns sensitivity analysis from descriptive into prescriptive/actionable output
Geometry-conditioned sensitivity narrative: report not just 'how sensitive' but 'sensitive depending on what structural feature' (loop vs linear course) - elevates analysis from rote to insightful
Image-to-data digitization with Python pixel reading to manufacture model inputs from published maps when no numeric dataset exists

Stakeholder-facing deliverable: close with a persuasive, jargon-light letter that states the headline number, names the critical decision points, and pre-empts real-world execution obstacles (here: rider can't sense wattage -> propose sensors + headset coaching)
